goal: Predict bulk_modulus, density, tg for PVNL (*CC(Cl)*)
assumptions: PVC is atactic (amorphous); syndiotactic PVC has measurably higher Tg but atactic is standard for amorphous MD
PVC is glassy at 300 K (experimental Tg ~354 K, MD Tg expected 380-470 K >> 300 K)
PCFF embeds bond-increment partial charges; no separate QM charge job required
Temperature parameters from polymer_rules.json PVNL class entry are validated for this class (confidence=medium)

uncertainties.name: ff_transferability
uncertainties.description: PCFF/COMPASS for PVC has medium-confidence validation (class-specific Tg/density papers exist but no run-0 parity for this exact cell yet)

uncertainties.name: hardware_optimum
uncertainties.description: directional_probe was measured on A800 40GB / 32-core host; live host is RTX 6000 24GB / 18-core; values_are_benchmarked=false — by_forcefield[pcff] default used (engine=kokkos, mpi=1, gpu_per_run=1)

evidence.claim: EMC/PCFF build-tested for PVC PASS, PVAc PASS, PVA PASS. OPLS-AA failed for PVC and PVAc; PCFF succeeded. PCFF has full parameter coverage for C, H, Cl atom types in vinyl chloride backbone.
PCFF/COMPASS validated for polyvinyl class Tg and density
PCFF class2 force field validated for chlorinated polymers
MD Tg expected 380-470 K for PVC after ~80-120 K overestimation — consistent with PCFF class2 systematic Tg overestimation pattern

alternatives.name: OPLS-AA
alternatives.reason_rejected: OPLS-AA failed EMC build for PVC and PVAc (no valid dihedral parameters for C-C-C-Cl backbone); PCFF succeeded — builder constraint

alternatives.name: GAFF2_mod
alternatives.reason_rejected: RadonPy/GAFF2_mod path retired; Tg error >45% documented for vinyl class; replaced by EMC/PCFF pipeline

alternatives.name: TraPPE-UA
alternatives.reason_rejected: TraPPE-UA is united-atom and lacks explicit Cl atom type; cannot represent polar C-Cl side chain charges that dominate PVC electrostatics

evidence.claim: PCFF embeds bond-increment charges (BIC) for C-Cl and C-H bonds; partial charges are computed automatically by EMC from the PCFF force field parameters. No separate QM charge job is required or appropriate for PCFF.
PVC has significant partial charges on backbone carbons from C-Cl bond polarity; PPPM is required (see D-03). PCFF BIC captures this without QM.

alternatives.name: AM1-BCC
alternatives.reason_rejected: Only appropriate for the retired RadonPy/GAFF path; inconsistent with PCFF BIC framework

evidence.claim: PVC contains Cl heteroatom on every repeat unit backbone carbon. C-Cl bond polarity gives |q| >> 0.1e on backbone carbons. PPPM required per electrostatics_decision_guide rule: heteroatom-containing or |q|>0.1e backbones MUST use pppm.
Neglecting long-range Coulomb biases density/Tg by 5-20% for polar polymers; PVC chloride gives significant partial charges on backbone — PPPM required.

alternatives.name: lj_cut only
alternatives.known_error: 5-20% density/Tg bias for polar polymers with |q|>0.1e; rejected by electrostatics_decision_guide hard rule

evidence.claim: DP=60 >> Fox-Flory plateau (DP>=20) for Tg; above entanglement-MW threshold for bulk modulus. PVC entanglement MW ~18-24 kg/mol; DP=60 gives ~3750 g/mol per chain x 10 chains = sufficient statistical ensemble.
Estimated cell atoms: 6 atoms/monomer (all-atom: 2C + 1Cl + 2H + 1H) × 60 DP × 10 chains = 3600 atoms. Well above 12A cutoff self-imaging threshold; within 1 GPU budget.
nchain=10 provides adequate chain statistics for Rg and end-to-end vector distributions.

alternatives.name: DP=30, nchain=10
alternatives.reason_rejected: dp_min=30 is viable but gives fewer chain statistics; DP=60 is preferred for Tg/BM accuracy with adequate GPU budget

evidence.claim: PVC is glassy at T_equil_K=530K (above Tg) during melt equilibration; DP=60 chains satisfy require_glassy carve-out. Melt chain self-diffusion is unattainable on MD timescales for DP=60 PVC (measured tau_relax ~3.6e9 ps); overall_pass=True is unsatisfiable by construction. Gate on density plateau + homogeneity CV<0.25 + P2 nematic<0.10; melt chain metrics are advisory.
5-cycle high-T anneal (annealing_T_high_K=610K) substitutes for melt self-diffusion in relaxing chain conformations.

decisions.choice: T sweep 150-550 K, step 20 K, rates [40,160,400] K/ns, bilinear R²>=0.80

evidence.claim: Experimental PVC Tg ~354 K (80°C). Sweep range 150-550 K brackets [354 K] with 200 K margin below and 196 K above. MD Tg expected 380-470 K; upper bound 550 K gives ≥80 K margin above expected MD Tg.
Step 20 K gives (550-150)/20 = 20 temperature bins — well above the 4-bin minimum. 500k steps/T at rate 40 K/ns = 12.5 ns/step, total ~250 ns for slowest rate.
3-rate multirate protocol [40, 160, 400] K/ns enables Williams-Landel-Ferry extrapolation to lab cooling rate; slowest rate 40 K/ns clears tg_min_steps_per_T=200k (12.5 ns/step).

alternatives.name: single-rate sweep
alternatives.reason_rejected: Single rate cannot extrapolate to experimental cooling rate; multirate WLF extrapolation gives more defensible Tg estimate

decisions.choice: glassy Murnaghan NPT ±1000 atm at 300 K (primary); 3-direction uniaxial deformation fallback

evidence.claim: PVC is glassy at 300 K (exp Tg ~354 K, MD Tg ~380-470 K >> 300 K). Glassy BM via Murnaghan NPT compression at 300 K — symmetric ±1000 atm pressure series on npt_prod300_out.data. Wu (2019) validates NPT compression as faster and more reliable than volume-fluctuation for polymer K.

Born+NVT REMOVED 2026-06-21: PCFF+PPPM virial incompatibility confirmed across PMMA4, PVC1, PEEK1 — K_Born 8-15x too high, Var(P) ~10^7x too high, failed 3/3 pipeline runs. This exact polymer (PVC1) was one of the three failure cases.
Experimental K_T for glassy PVC at 25°C: 3.5-4.5 GPa (Mark 2007 Table 7.6 Tait params, b0=3751 bar → K_T≈4.0 GPa; consistent with K_S=[4.0,5.0]×0.87 revision range).

alternatives.name: Born+NVT
alternatives.known_error: K_Born 8-15x too high for PCFF+PPPM; failed 3/3 runs (PMMA4, PVC1, PEEK1 2026-06-21) — REMOVED from pipeline

alternatives.name: 3-direction uniaxial deformation
alternatives.role: fallback if Murnaghan fit_converged=False or B0_prime outside [4,20]

evidence.claim: FF family = pcff (PCFF class2 + PPPM). by_forcefield[pcff] default: engine=kokkos, mpi=1, gpu_per_run=1. KOKKOS full-offload validated for PCFF: 7.9x vs GPU-package A0 baseline (34.9 ns/day at 1 rank contended).

Estimated cell: 3600 atoms (6 atoms/monomer × 60 DP × 10 chains). Benchmark cell: 3020 atoms (CALIB_PCFF). Ratio 3600/3020 = 1.19 — within [0.5x, 2x] of benchmark cell. However, directional_probe.measured_on = A800 40GB/32-core host; live host = RTX 6000 24GB/18-core. Host mismatch AND values_are_benchmarked=false → cannot adopt recommended_by_ff; must use by_forcefield default.
Cell 3600 < 10k atoms → 1 GPU confirmed. mpi=1 is canonical for engine=kokkos (kspace runs on GPU, not CPU-serial); the 'never mpi=1' rule applies only to gpu-package, not KOKKOS.
by_forcefield[pcff] default = engine=kokkos, mpi=1, gpu_per_run=1. Choice equals default → no hardware keys written to decided_params (keeps worker prompt identical to policy path).

decisions.note: values_are_benchmarked=false + host mismatch; hardware_benchmark probe planned to validate on live RTX 6000 host
